acceptance_criteria: The USER regime no longer explodes through uninterpretable interest-transfer feedback, or the module is explicitly retired from evidentiary use.
The ABM austerity path either binds in a measurable, directional way or is documented as exploratory only.
Any repaired model comes with adversarial tests that validate the claimed directional behavior.
constraints: Low-priority research task; not required to publish the current draft.
Do not weaken the current audit boundaries until new validation exists.
description: Investigate the issuer-vs-user SFC feedback problem and the inert ABM austerity channel; produce interpretable replacements or retire those surfaces explicitly.
goal: Repair or replace the legacy SFC and ABM surfaces before they are ever used again as evidence in the post.
implementation_notes: Superseded by the refined research-track split: task-010 fragility robustness, task-011 adversarial test hardening, task-012 ABM redesign, task-013 issuer/user SFC redesign, and task-014 inflation/resource-boundary modeling.
title: Future repair of legacy money-minsky-policy-mismatch models
